Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for threads related to "Sunbelt" or "invoice" or "Palmetto" (or similar) using search_emails to discover the invoice swap incident specifically that Hana Kim sent the April 10 invoice batch where Sunbelt Venture Partners (INV-2026-0398, $4,800) received Palmetto Community Foundation's nonprofit-rate PDF ($2,400), and Palmetto (INV-2026-0399, $2,400) received Sunbelt's commercial-rate PDF ($4,800). Key emails include Raj Greenfield questioning the Palmetto header on his invoice, Carmen Delgado-Reyes disputing the overbilled amount and raising the May 15 nonprofit audit deadline, Emeka Diallo's correction emails to both clients, and Marcus Thorne's remediation directives.
OE 2: Search emails for David Chen's internal message comparing the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident to the Terraform pricing leak using search_emails with a query related to "pricing exposure" or "Terraform" or "Sunbelt" (or similar). The agent should discover email email_email_7fe9a06077f6, subject "Treating Sunbelt/Palmetto as repeat pricing-exposure incident after Terraform," sent by david.chen@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com, samira.tariq@moveops.com, emeka.diallo@moveops.com, and hana.kim@moveops.com. This email explicitly frames the Sunbelt/Palmetto swap as the second pricing-exposure event after the Terraform reply-all incident.
OE 3: Search emails for Victoria Chen-Nakamura's escalation email using search_emails with a query related to "Victoria" or "portfolio pricing" or "Sunbelt" (or similar) to discover email email_email_68e6dbbcc583 from victoria.chen-nakamura@sunbeltvp.com to david.chen@moveops.com (CC raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com), subject "Portfolio pricing conversation for Sunbelt companies." This email shows the Sunbelt COO is now asking about portfolio-wide pricing for Sunbelt-backed companies, driven by seeing the $2,400 Palmetto invoice.
OE 4: Search emails for the Palmetto nonprofit audit concern using search_emails with a query related to "Palmetto" or "Carmen" or "audit" (or similar) to discover that Carmen Delgado-Reyes (carmen.delgado-reyes@palmettofoundation.org) needs corrected documentation before the May 15, 2026 nonprofit audit deadline. Emeka's reply (email_email_cf05b9a123a0) provides a written correction explaining INV-2026-0399 for $2,400 is the correct invoice.
OE 5: Search Slack messages for discussions about the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice incident and the pricing exposure pattern using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "pricing exposure" (or similar). Key messages include David Chen's message in C002 (#customer-engagement) framing this as a second pricing-exposure incident after Terraform, Emeka's reconstruction in C002 confirming the PDFs were crossed, and Marcus Thorne's EOD expectations in C005 (#finance) demanding corrected communications, a Sunbelt recovery plan, and engineering reactivation of the attachment-mismatch safeguard.
OE 6: Search emails for the StormCloud CFO escalation using search_emails with a query related to "StormCloud" or "Liam" or "leadership review" (or similar) to discover Liam Park's (CFO, liam.park@stormcloud.io) email to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com (email_email_7b0cc9a6d376), subject "Urgent before tomorrow's leadership review." This email lists four unresolved issues: (1) Jae-won Kim still in temporary housing with unsigned lease, (2) the $500 goodwill credit not appearing on their invoice, (3) Simone Richter's $340 standing desk reimbursement stuck in the stipend freeze, and (4) the Ridgeline Robotics referral from CEO Anya Petrova that went unanswered.
OE 7: Search emails for Mina's internal reconstruction and recovery plan for StormCloud using search_emails with a query related to "StormCloud" or "Mina" or "Jae-won" (or similar) to discover: (a) Mina's team reconstruction email (email_email_1a723752e25f) detailing the chain of failures, (b) Julian's reply explaining the $500 credit was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, (c) Marcus's QuickBooks confirmation of the same accounting error, and (d) Mina's recovery plan email to Liam (email_email_1179abf605df) covering all four items.
OE 8: Search Slack messages for StormCloud-related discussions using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" or "Ridgeline" (or similar) to find: Marcus's QuickBooks confirmation in C005 that the $500 was applied to EST-2026-0287 not INV-2026-0319, Julian's client framing in C007 (#customer-support), David's admission in C002 that the Ridgeline referral was celebrated but never actioned (no CRM deal, Kenji's 4/21 follow-up unanswered), and Mina's recovery plan in C004 (#executive).
OE 9: Search CRM for StormCloud Analytics company record using crm_search_companies with name "StormCloud" (or similar) to retrieve the company record (company_stormcloud), confirming Mina Hashimoto as AM, 3 completed relocations at $8,800 each ($26,400 total), and the Feb 24 quality complaint.
OE 10: Search CRM for StormCloud deals using crm_search_deals with a query related to "StormCloud" (or similar) to find deal_stormcloud_won ($26,400, closedWon) and any other active deals, providing deal-level context for the account.
OE 11: Search CRM for BrightLoop Analytics company record using crm_search_companies with name "BrightLoop" (or similar) to retrieve company_brightloop, confirming Mina Hashimoto as AM, 145 employees, $28M revenue, and the expansion potential.
OE 12: Search CRM for BrightLoop deals using crm_search_deals with a query related to "BrightLoop" (or similar) to find deal_brightloop_q2 (Jordan Ekwueme, $7,000, qualifiedtobuy), deal_brightloop_apr2026 (Simone Richter + Marcus Webb, $11,350, closedwon with details about Rush surcharge, vehicle shipping, INV-2026-0308 due May 2).
OE 13: Search emails for BrightLoop expansion request and first batch problems using search_emails with a query related to "BrightLoop" or "Tessa Moreno" or "Boston relocations" or "Series B" (or similar) to discover: (a) Tessa Moreno's April 22 request for six new Boston relocations after BrightLoop's Series B, (b) problems from the first/current batch including Simone Richter's UrbanNest placement being a studio instead of the promised 1BR, and (c) Marcus Webb's Road Runner Auto Transport delay with no response to his follow-up emails about the Honda Civic delivery ETA.
OE 14: Search Slack messages for BrightLoop-related discussions using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "BrightLoop" or "Tessa" or "Series B" (or similar) to find internal context on the expansion request and any discussion of the first batch problems.
OE 15: Search or list Linear issues related to BrightLoop using linear_list_issues with a query related to "BrightLoop" (or similar) to discover linear_issue_c16357d188c6 ("BrightLoop account audit: reopen unresolved April relocations before May expansion") which documents: Tessa's April 22 expansion request for six new Boston relocations, Simone's housing mismatch (studio vs 1BR), Marcus Webb's vehicle delay, and CRM overstating the April batch as completed/smooth. Also discover linear_issue_f85be674c9b8 ("Document BrightLoop ops gaps") listing Marcus vendor miss and Simone housing issues.
OE 16: Search or list Linear issues related to the invoice attachment safeguard using linear_list_issues with a query related to "invoice" or "attachment" or "safeguard" (or similar) to discover linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2 ("Invoice email template safety checks prevent attachment mismatches"), issue number 187, team_engineering, state_id state_backlog, created 2026-03-18. This is the engineering safeguard ticket raised after the Terraform pricing leak that was deprioritized on April 6 and never shipped. Also find linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130 ("Reconstruct Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment exposure and issue corrected documentation"), issue 417, which cross-references the safeguard ticket.
OE 17: Search emails for the engineering safeguard context using search_emails with a query related to "safeguard" or "attachment" or "Terraform" (or similar) to discover Samira Tariq's email (email_email_9b0b7e4db5aa), subject "Engineering context: dormant invoice attachment safeguard after Terraform," which confirms that engineering identified this risk after Terraform, opened a ticket, Lena deprioritized it on April 6, no interim production safeguard existed, and the Sunbelt/Palmetto problem is the direct consequence.
OE 18: Look up contact details for Elena Rostova using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts with a query related to "Elena" (or similar) to retrieve her email address elena.rostova@moveops.com for the briefing email recipient.
OE 19: Look up contact details for David Chen and Marcus Thorne using contacts_search_contacts with queries related to "David Chen" and "Marcus Thorne" (or similar) to retrieve david.chen@moveops.com and marcus.thorne@moveops.com for the calendar meeting attendees.
OE 20: Look up the Slack channel ID for #executive using channels_list to find channel C004 for posting the heads-up message.
OE 21 [WRITE ACTION]: Send a briefing email to Elena Rostova from Priya Chakrabarti using send_email with sender priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com, recipient elena.rostova@moveops.com. The email content must cover all three situations with facts:
Situation 1 - Sunbelt/Palmetto Invoice Swap & Pricing Exposure:
- On April 10, Hana Kim's batch invoice send attached the wrong PDFs: Sunbelt (INV-2026-0398, $4,800) received Palmetto's nonprofit-rate document ($2,400), and Palmetto (INV-2026-0399, $2,400) received Sunbelt's commercial document ($4,800)
- Raj Greenfield (Sunbelt) questioned whether the lower rate was available to Sunbelt
- Victoria Chen-Nakamura (Sunbelt COO) has escalated to David, asking about portfolio-wide pricing for Sunbelt-backed companies
- Carmen Delgado-Reyes (Palmetto) needs corrected documentation before the May 15 nonprofit audit deadline
- This is the second pricing-exposure incident after the Terraform reply-all
Situation 2- StormCloud CFO Escalation:
- Liam Park (CFO) sent an aggressive escalation email to Mina listing four unresolved items
- Jae-won Kim is still in temporary housing the Austin lease was never finalized with UrbanNest (guarantor paperwork issue)
- The $500 goodwill credit (from the Jamie Reeves flight booking error) was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319
- Simone Richter's $340 standing desk reimbursement is stuck in the company-wide stipend freeze
- Anya Petrova's warm referral to Ridgeline Robotics (Kenji Watanabe, HR Director, 5 Austin relocations) went unanswered until April 24
Situation 3 - BrightLoop Expansion vs. Unresolved Problems:
- Tessa Moreno requested six new Boston relocations after BrightLoop's Series B
- First/current batch has open service failures: Simone Richter received a studio instead of promised 1BR from UrbanNest, Marcus Webb's Road Runner vehicle delivery is delayed with no response to follow-ups
- CRM engagement notes overstate the April batch as smooth/completed vs reality
The email must explicitly flag the pricing exposure pattern (Terraform + Sunbelt/Palmetto) as systemic.
OE 22 [WRITE ACTION]: Create a CRM engagement note for StormCloud Analytics using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids including "company_stormcloud", and body covering: Liam Park's CFO escalation, Jae-won Kim's unresolved temporary housing, the $500 goodwill credit accounting error (EST-2026-0287 vs INV-2026-0319), Simone Richter's frozen reimbursement, and the Ridgeline referral that went unanswered until late recovery.
OE 23 [WRITE ACTION]: Create a CRM engagement note for BrightLoop Analytics using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids including "company_brightloop", and body covering: Tessa Moreno's six-Boston-relocation expansion request, the unresolved problems from the current batch (Simone Richter studio vs 1BR housing mismatch, Marcus Webb vehicle shipping delay), and the gap between CRM-recorded status and actual service delivery status.
OE 24 [WRITE ACTION]: Add a comment to the invoice attachment safeguard Linear ticket using linear_create_comment with issueId "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2" (or the agent may discover the issue ID dynamically) and body noting that a second pricing-exposure incident (Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice PDF swap on April 10) has occurred before the safeguard fix was shipped, referencing the Terraform incident as the first occurrence.
OE 25 [WRITE ACTION]: Schedule a 30-minute calendar meeting on April 28, 2026 using calendar_add_calendar_event with title related to "Pricing Exposure" or "Account Risks" or "Invoice Incident" (or similar), start_datetime on 2026-04-28, duration of 30 minutes, and attendees including elena.rostova@moveops.com, david.chen@moveops.com, and marcus.thorne@moveops.com. The meeting is to discuss the pricing exposure pattern and account risks.
OE 26 [WRITE ACTION]: Post a heads-up message in the #executive Slack channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C004" and payload informing leadership that Elena will be asking about the Sunbelt/Palmetto pricing exposure, the StormCloud CFO escalation, and the BrightLoop expansion issues, so nobody is caught off guard.
OE 27: Search Airtable relocation records for StormCloud using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records to find Jae-won Kim's record (recJaeWonKimStormCloudAustin), which shows Status "In Progress" but Special Requirements note says "Lease still unsigned UrbanNest flagged landlord holdout on guarantor paperwork. Record incorrectly shows Completed." Also find the three completed StormCloud relocations (Jamie Reeves, Sasha Petrov, Colin Nakamura) including the flight booking incident on Jamie Reeves' record.
OE 28: Search Airtable relocation records for BrightLoop using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records to find records for Simone Richter (recSimoneRichterBrightloop, rush/temp housing) and Marcus Webb (recMarcusWebbBrightloop, vehicle shipping pending BrightLoop confirmation) to verify the status of the current batch and identify open service failures.
OE 29: Search Linear issues for StormCloud account context using linear_list_issues with a query related to "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" (or similar) to find linear_issue_c97359216490 ("StormCloud Relocation – Jae-won Kim – SEA → AUS," closed prematurely while Jae-won was still in temporary housing), linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0 ("Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents," deprioritized because Airtable showed status as Completed), and linear_issue_a49b4d199139 ("StormCloud account incident reconstruction," the cross-functional reconstruction ticket opened after Liam's escalation).
OE 30: Search or list QuickBooks invoices related to StormCloud using quickbooks_search_invoices to verify the $500 goodwill credit accounting error confirming that the credit was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the active invoice INV-2026-0319.
OE 31: Search CRM for the Ridgeline Robotics lead/deal using crm_search_companies or crm_search_leads or crm_search_deals with a query related to "Ridgeline" (or similar) to find crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2 (Ridgeline Robotics, Prospect), crm_deal_a95b4721982a ($8,750, "Qualified To Buy," initial outreach unanswered for days), and crm_lead_de04045463c3 (Kenji Watanabe, HR Director, referred by Anya Petrova on 2026-04-18, follow-up 2026-04-21 unanswered until 2026-04-24). This confirms whether the referral is being handled.
OE 32: Search emails for the Ridgeline referral chain using search_emails with a query related to "Ridgeline" or "Kenji" or "Anya" (or similar) to find the warm introduction email from Anya Petrova, Kenji Watanabe's follow-up (April 21), David Chen's late recovery reply (email_email_57faf42664a0, April 24), and Mina's reply to Kenji (email_email_a0cae8e27470) offering meeting times.
OE 33: Search emails or Slack for the Terraform reply-all incident details using search_emails with a query related to "Terraform" or "reply-all" or "Catalina" (or similar), or look up the CRM engagement (engagement-terraform-replyall-incident) using crm_list_engagements, to understand the first pricing-exposure incident: Catalina Dubois accidentally Reply-All'd internal pricing strategy to Rachel Nguyen at Terraform Digital, exposing the 25% margin floor and tiered volume discount structure.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for threads related to "Sunbelt" or "invoice" or "Palmetto" (or similar) using search_emails to discover the invoice swap incident specifically that Hana Kim sent the April 10 invoice batch where Sunbelt Venture Partners (INV-2026-0398, $4,800) received Palmetto Community Foundation's nonprofit-rate PDF ($2,400), and Palmetto (INV-2026-0399, $2,400) received Sunbelt's commercial-rate PDF ($4,800). Key emails include Raj Greenfield questioning the Palmetto header on his invoice, Carmen Delgado-Reyes disputing the overbilled amount and raising the May 15 nonprofit audit deadline, Emeka Diallo's correction emails to both clients, and Marcus Thorne's remediation directives.
OE 2: Search emails for David Chen's internal message comparing the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident to the Terraform pricing leak using search_emails with a query related to "pricing exposure" or "Terraform" or "Sunbelt" (or similar). OE 7: Search emails for Mina's internal reconstruction and recovery plan for StormCloud using search_emails with a query related to "StormCloud" or "Mina" or "Jae-won" (or similar) to discover: (a) Mina's team reconstruction email (email_email_1a723752e25f) detailing the chain of failures, (b) Julian's reply explaining the $500 credit was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319, (c) Marcus's QuickBooks confirmation of the same accounting error, and (d) Mina's recovery plan email to Liam (email_email_1179abf605df) covering all four items.

OE 10: Search CRM for StormCloud deals using crm_search_deals with a query related to "StormCloud" (or similar) to find deal_stormcloud_won ($26,400, closedWon) and any other active deals, providing deal-level context for the account.
OE 11: Search CRM for BrightLoop Analytics company record using crm_search_companies with name "BrightLoop" (or similar) to retrieve company_brightloop, confirming Mina Hashimoto as AM, 145 employees, $28M revenue, and the expansion potential.
OE 12: Search CRM for BrightLoop deals using crm_search_deals with a query related to "BrightLoop" (or similar) to find deal_brightloop_q2 (Jordan Ekwueme, $7,000, qualifiedtobuy), deal_brightloop_apr2026 (Simone Richter + Marcus Webb, $11,350, closedwon with details about Rush surcharge, vehicle shipping, INV-2026-0308 due May 2).
OE 13: Search emails for BrightLoop expansion request and first batch problems using search_emails with a query related to "BrightLoop" or "Tessa Moreno" or "Boston relocations" or "Series B" (or similar) to discover: (a) Tessa Moreno's April 22 request for six new Boston relocations after BrightLoop's Series B, (b) problems from the first/current batch including Simone Richter's UrbanNest placement being a studio instead of the promised 1BR, and (c) Marcus Webb's Road Runner Auto Transport delay with no response to his follow-up emails about the Honda Civic delivery ETA.
OE 14: Search Slack messages for BrightLoop-related discussions using conversations_search_messages with a query related to "BrightLoop" or "Tessa" or "Series B" (or similar) to find internal context on the expansion request and any discussion of the first batch problems.
OE 15: Search or list Linear issues related to BrightLoop using linear_list_issues with a query related to "BrightLoop" (or similar) to discover linear_issue_c16357d188c6 ("BrightLoop account audit: reopen unresolved April relocations before May expansion") which documents: Tessa's April 22 expansion request for six new Boston relocations, Simone's housing mismatch (studio vs 1BR), Marcus Webb's vehicle delay, and CRM overstating the April batch as completed/smooth. Also discover linear_issue_f85be674c9b8 ("Document BrightLoop ops gaps") listing Marcus vendor miss and Simone housing issues.
OE 16: Search or list Linear issues related to the invoice attachment safeguard using linear_list_issues with a query related to "invoice" or "attachment" or "safeguard" (or similar) to discover linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2 ("Invoice email template safety checks prevent attachment mismatches"), issue number 187, team_engineering, state_id state_backlog, created 2026-03-18. This is the engineering safeguard ticket raised after the Terraform pricing leak that was deprioritized on April 6 and never shipped. Also find linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130 ("Reconstruct Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment exposure and issue corrected documentation"), issue 417, which cross-references the safeguard ticket.
OE 17: Search emails for the engineering safeguard context using search_emails with a query related to "safeguard" or "attachment" or "Terraform" (or similar) to discover Samira Tariq's email (email_email_9b0b7e4db5aa), subject "Engineering context: dormant invoice attachment safeguard after Terraform," which confirms that engineering identified this risk after Terraform, opened a ticket, Lena deprioritized it on April 6, no interim production safeguard existed, and the Sunbelt/Palmetto problem is the direct consequence.
OE 18: Look up contact details for Elena Rostova using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts with a query related to "Elena" (or similar) to retrieve her email address elena.rostova@moveops.com for the briefing email recipient.
OE 19: Look up contact details for David Chen and Marcus Thorne using contacts_search_contacts with queries related to "David Chen" and "Marcus Thorne" (or similar) to retrieve david.chen@moveops.com and marcus.thorne@moveops.com for the calendar meeting attendees.
OE 20: Look up the Slack channel ID for #executive using channels_list to find channel C004 for posting the heads-up message.
OE 21 [WRITE ACTION]: Send a briefing email to Elena Rostova from Priya Chakrabarti using send_email with sender priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com, recipient elena.rostova@moveops.com. The email content must cover all three situations with facts:
Situation 1 - Sunbelt/Palmetto Invoice Swap & Pricing Exposure:
- On April 10, Hana Kim's batch invoice send attached the wrong PDFs: Sunbelt (INV-2026-0398, $4,800) received Palmetto's nonprofit-rate document ($2,400), and Palmetto (INV-2026-0399, $2,400) received Sunbelt's commercial document ($4,800)
- Raj Greenfield (Sunbelt) questioned whether the lower rate was available to Sunbelt
- Victoria Chen-Nakamura (Sunbelt COO) has escalated to David, asking about portfolio-wide pricing for Sunbelt-backed companies
- Carmen Delgado-Reyes (Palmetto) needs corrected documentation before the May 15 nonprofit audit deadline
- This is the second pricing-exposure incident after the Terraform reply-all
Situation 2- StormCloud CFO Escalation:
- Liam Park (CFO) sent an aggressive escalation email to Mina listing four unresolved items
- Jae-won Kim is still in temporary housing the Austin lease was never finalized with UrbanNest (guarantor paperwork issue)
- The $500 goodwill credit (from the Jamie Reeves flight booking error) was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of active invoice INV-2026-0319
- Simone Richter's $340 standing desk reimbursement is stuck in the company-wide stipend freeze
- Anya Petrova's warm referral to Ridgeline Robotics (Kenji Watanabe, HR Director, 5 Austin relocations) went unanswered until April 24
Situation 3 - BrightLoop Expansion vs. Unresolved Problems:
- Tessa Moreno requested six new Boston relocations after BrightLoop's Series B
- First/current batch has open service failures: Simone Richter received a studio instead of promised 1BR from UrbanNest, Marcus Webb's Road Runner vehicle delivery is delayed with no response to follow-ups
- CRM engagement notes overstate the April batch as smooth/completed vs reality
The email must explicitly flag the pricing exposure pattern (Terraform + Sunbelt/Palmetto) as systemic.
OE 22 [WRITE ACTION]: Create a CRM engagement note for StormCloud Analytics using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids including "company_stormcloud", and body covering: Liam Park's CFO escalation, Jae-won Kim's unresolved temporary housing, the $500 goodwill credit accounting error (EST-2026-0287 vs INV-2026-0319), Simone Richter's frozen reimbursement, and the Ridgeline referral that went unanswered until late recovery.
OE 23 [WRITE ACTION]: Create a CRM engagement note for BrightLoop Analytics using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE", company_ids including "company_brightloop", and body covering: Tessa Moreno's six-Boston-relocation expansion request, the unresolved problems from the current batch (Simone Richter studio vs 1BR housing mismatch, Marcus Webb vehicle shipping delay), and the gap between CRM-recorded status and actual service delivery status.
OE 24 [WRITE ACTION]: Add a comment to the invoice attachment safeguard Linear ticket using linear_create_comment with issueId "linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2" (or the agent may discover the issue ID dynamically) and body noting that a second pricing-exposure incident (Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice PDF swap on April 10) has occurred before the safeguard fix was shipped, referencing the Terraform incident as the first occurrence.
OE 25 [WRITE ACTION]: Schedule a 30-minute calendar meeting on April 28, 2026 using calendar_add_calendar_event with title related to "Pricing Exposure" or "Account Risks" or "Invoice Incident" (or similar), start_datetime on 2026-04-28, duration of 30 minutes, and attendees including elena.rostova@moveops.com, david.chen@moveops.com, and marcus.thorne@moveops.com. The meeting is to discuss the pricing exposure pattern and account risks.
OE 26 [WRITE ACTION]: Post a heads-up message in the #executive Slack channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C004" and payload informing leadership that Elena will be asking about the Sunbelt/Palmetto pricing exposure, the StormCloud CFO escalation, and the BrightLoop expansion issues, so nobody is caught off guard.

Record incorrectly shows Completed." Also find the three completed StormCloud relocations (Jamie Reeves, Sasha Petrov, Colin Nakamura) including the flight booking incident on Jamie Reeves' record.
OE 28: Search Airtable relocation records for BrightLoop using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records to find records for Simone Richter (recSimoneRichterBrightloop, rush/temp housing) and Marcus Webb (recMarcusWebbBrightloop, vehicle shipping pending BrightLoop confirmation) to verify the status of the current batch and identify open service failures.
OE 29: Search Linear issues for StormCloud account context using linear_list_issues with a query related to "StormCloud" or "Jae-won" (or similar) to find linear_issue_c97359216490 ("StormCloud Relocation – Jae-won Kim – SEA → AUS," closed prematurely while Jae-won was still in temporary housing), linear_issue_e7bcaf6978f0 ("Follow up with Jae-won Kim on requested Austin lease documents," deprioritized because Airtable showed status as Completed), and linear_issue_a49b4d199139 ("StormCloud account incident reconstruction," the cross-functional reconstruction ticket opened after Liam's escalation).
OE 30: Search or list QuickBooks invoices related to StormCloud using quickbooks_search_invoices to verify the $500 goodwill credit accounting error confirming that the credit was posted to cancelled estimate EST-2026-0287 instead of the active invoice INV-2026-0319.
OE 31: Search CRM for the Ridgeline Robotics lead/deal using crm_search_companies or crm_search_leads or crm_search_deals with a query related to "Ridgeline" (or similar) to find crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2 (Ridgeline Robotics, Prospect), crm_deal_a95b4721982a ($8,750, "Qualified To Buy," initial outreach unanswered for days), and crm_lead_de04045463c3 (Kenji Watanabe, HR Director, referred by Anya Petrova on 2026-04-18, follow-up 2026-04-21 unanswered until 2026-04-24). This confirms whether the referral is being handled.
OE 32: Search emails for the Ridgeline referral chain using search_emails with a query related to "Ridgeline" or "Kenji" or "Anya" (or similar) to find the warm introduction email from Anya Petrova, Kenji Watanabe's follow-up (April 21), David Chen's late recovery reply (email_email_57faf42664a0, April 24), and Mina's reply to Kenji (email_email_a0cae8e27470) offering meeting times.
OE 33: Search emails or Slack for the Terraform reply-all incident details using search_emails with a query related to "Terraform" or "reply-all" or "Catalina" (or similar), or look up the CRM engagement (engagement-terraform-replyall-incident) using crm_list_engagements, to understand the first pricing-exposure incident: Catalina Dubois accidentally Reply-All'd internal pricing strategy to Rachel Nguyen at Terraform Digital, exposing the 25% margin floor and tiered volume discount structure.
